author: [{'author': 'The name of the author of the respective document, written in a bold, black font, centered on the page, located at the top of the page, below the title of the document, and followed by the author’s degree and the institution where the work was conducted.'}]
chapter: [{'chapter': 'A chapter title is a large, bold, and prominent text that introduces a new section or chapter in a document, typically indicating the main topic or subject matter being discussed in that section, and is usually placed at the beginning of a new page. It is distinct from mathematical notations, equations, and code snippets, and is visually larger and more prominent than a footnote. Additionally, it is placed at the beginning of a new page, is usually surrounded by a border or a box, and is visually larger and more prominent than a section title or a list of tables. It is also typically centered on the page and is the first text on the page, unlike the "List of Figures" which is a table of contents for figures and is not a chapter title. The chapter title is a distinct and separate entity from the list of references, which is a table of contents for a list of sources cited in the document. Furthermore, a chapter title is not typically associated with scientific or technical content, such as the detailed experimental results and data presented in a section discussing a specific topic within the chapter.'}]
equation: [{'equation': 'The equation class objects are structured mathematical expressions that are presented in a clear, standard mathematical notation, often involving variables, constants, and operators. They represent specific mathematical relationships or concepts and are typically numbered for easy referencing. The equations are distinct from other elements in the scene due to their mathematical content, equation format, mathematical symbols, equation structure, and mathematical notation. The equations must be a structured mathematical expression with a clear format, symbols, and structure, and they must be distinct from tables, figures, or other non-mathematical content in the document.'}]
equation number: [{'equation number': 'A standalone equation number is a single mathematical expression (e.g., Eq 3.5, Eq 2.15) that is visually separated from the surrounding text by distinct formatting and numbering, is enclosed in square brackets, is a mathematical expression, and is not a section title or a table. The equation number is typically located at the top right corner of the equation and is a part of the equation itself, not a separate label. The equation number is used to reference the equation in the text and is not a standalone object in the text. The equation number is visually distinct from the text and is not part of a larger mathematical expression or a section title. The equation number is enclosed in square brackets and is located at the top right corner of the equation.'}]
figure: [{'figure': 'A visual representation that illustrates a scientific concept or process, accompanied by text that explains the data or process, and may include line graphs (e.g., polarization curves for fuel cells) or chemical structure diagrams (e.g., synthesis pathways for chemical compounds). The figure should not include tables of statistical distributions and parameters, and should not be a table of material properties or other tabular data. The figure should not contain tables of statistical distributions and parameters, and should not be a table of material properties or other tabular data.'}]
figure caption: [{'figure caption': 'A descriptive text that provides a detailed explanation of the figure, including its title, the visual elements it highlights, and the context or findings associated with the figure. The text should be structured to clearly convey the purpose and content of the figure, and it should be distinct from the abstract or title page of the document. The figure caption should not include equations or mathematical expressions. The caption should focus on the visual elements and the context of the figure, such as the type of data or the simulation used, and should not be a proof or a theorem statement. The caption should be specific to the figure and should not be a general statement about the document. The figure caption should not be a chapter heading or a conclusion text, and it should not be a general statement about the document.'}]
footnote: [{'footnote': 'A textual reference or citation placed in a superscript number at the end of a sentence or paragraph, formatted in a specific style such as superscript numbers or italics, and used to attribute information to a source, enclosed in a green box, and used to convey additional information or context to the reader.'}]
list of content heading: [{'list of content heading': 'A structured page that lists the titles and page numbers of various tables or sections within a document, typically found at the beginning of a report or study. The page includes a prominent title at the top, such as "List of Tables," and a structured format with table titles and corresponding page numbers arranged in a consistent layout. The title is usually bold and centered, and the table titles are aligned to the left margin. The page number is positioned at the top right corner of the page, and the table titles are aligned to the left margin. The content heading should not include additional text or formatting that is not part of the table of contents, such as footnotes or explanatory text. The list of content heading should not include figures or their captions, as seen in the red bounding box. The page should not contain any other content, such as references or bibliographies, as seen in the red bounding box. The list of content heading should not include any other content, such as figures or their captions, as seen in the red bounding box.'}]
list of content text: [{'list of content text': 'A structured document that provides a numbered list of figures, each with a descriptive title and page number, aligned to the right. The list is typically found at the beginning of a document, such as a "List of Figures." The list of content text is distinct from other content text as it is specifically formatted for a list of figures, and it is not a mathematical or scientific equation. The content items in the list are visual representations or experimental results, such as SEM images, XRD patterns, or atomic structure models, rather than equations or text-based content. The list of content text in the green bounding box includes descriptive titles and page numbers aligned to the right, while the red bounding box contains a table with mean and standard deviation values for various sleep metrics, which is not part of the list of content text. The updated definition excludes tables with mean and standard deviation values and focuses on the visual and contextual features of the list of content text, ensuring that the list is not confused with other types of content such as text or figures with captions and legends.'}]
page number: [{'page number': 'A numerical value located at the bottom right corner of a page, typically indicating the current page number in a document, and is distinct from section headers, other text elements, and mathematical equation numbers. It is visually smaller and less prominent than the section header or title, and is usually accompanied by a page symbol (e.g., "Page 1", "1", "1/1", etc.). The page number is positioned in the bottom right corner of the page and is often accompanied by a page symbol. It is not associated with any mathematical equations or formulas, and it is not part of a section header or title.'}]
paragraph: [{'paragraph': 'A scholarly paragraph discussing a specific topic using experimental methods, technical details, and research findings, without delving into mathematical derivations or experimental setups, and excluding narrative or conversational text, equations, mathematical notations, lists of tables or figures, and tables of contents, and excluding dissertation title pages.'}]
reference text: [{'reference text': 'Structured references to scientific studies and research papers, formatted in a consistent manner, including author(s), title, journal or publication name, volume, issue, page numbers, and year of publication, written in a formal, academic style, discussing topics in fields such as science and technology, and mentioning specific research methods and peer-reviewed journals, excluding tables and numerical data, and excluding dissertation title pages.'}]
section: [{'section': 'A text-based document section that focuses on a specific topic, often involving discussion questions and responses from participants, and is structured with a title, subheading, and content presented in a narrative format. The section includes a section header, which is bold, larger font, numbered, and placed at the beginning of a new paragraph to introduce a new topic or subtopic. The section may or may not include a figure, and it should not contain any mathematical equations or figures that are part of a larger mathematical or scientific derivation. The section is a part of a text-based document and does not include a dissertation title page. The section is located within a larger document and is introduced with a section header that is bold, larger font, numbered, and placed at the beginning of a new paragraph. The section may include a figure and mathematical equations, but the equations should not be part of a larger mathematical or scientific derivation. The section may also include a discussion of the analytical form of the objective function, which involves the product of two Gaussian probability density functions and the derivation of the non-detection function.'}]
subsection: [{'subsection': 'A bolded, centered, and larger font size heading used to introduce a new subtopic within a document, providing a clear and concise overview of the topics discussed in that subtopic, and not containing a figure with a caption or a mathematical equation with a number in a red box, and not being a list of numbered items, and not being a figure caption or a mathematical equation with a number in a red box, and not being a section heading with a green bounding box, and not being a table, and not being a table of contents, and not being a dissertation title page.'}]
subsubsection: [{'subsubsection': 'A specific proposition or theory being discussed or tested in the text, written in formal, academic language, and often highlighted as a title or heading within the paper, excluding mathematical equations and figure captions, and is presented in a larger, bold, and green font, typically indicating a subsubsection of a section that focuses on a particular topic or methodological approach, and is underlined. The subsubsection is distinct from the table or figure captions as it is a textual proposition or theory, not a statistical summary or data table, and is not a list of contents or a table of figures. The subsubsection is further characterized by its content, which is often related to a specific analysis or discussion of a linguistic concept, such as aktionsart, and is typically found within the context of a larger discussion or argument in the text.'}]
table: Information organized in rows and columns.
table caption: [{'table caption': 'A structured title that precedes a table and provides a concise summary of the data presented in the table, often accompanied by column headers and rows of data, and is typically used to summarize and analyze data in a structured format, with a focus on the relationship between the table and the data it presents, and includes statistical significance indicators.'}]
table of contents text: [{'table of contents text': 'A structured list of headings and subheadings with page numbers, typically found at the beginning of a document, used to navigate the content. It is distinct from a table of contents text in that it is a textual representation of the document structure, whereas the table of contents text is a tabular representation of the same information. The table of contents text is organized in a hierarchical format, listing chapters, sections, and subsections with corresponding page numbers, and it is usually formatted in a consistent and standardized manner to facilitate easy navigation through the document. The updated class definition excludes lists of figures and their corresponding page numbers, as seen in the red bounding box, and includes the visual characteristics of the object in the green and blue bounding boxes, such as a structured list of headings and subheadings with page numbers, to better differentiate it from the "list of figures" class.'}]
title: [{'title': "A title page of a dissertation or thesis, prominently displayed in a larger font size, centered on the page, and including the title, author's name, university, and year of submission, with no mathematical equations or scientific text present, and no scientific figures or captions, and no text discussing citizenship as a legal status, and not a table of contents page, and not containing any scientific content or figures, and not including a section heading or subheading such as 'Likelihood free basics', and not a section heading or subheading such as '3.3 The corpus', and not containing any section headings or subheadings such as '4.2.1 Participants' or '4.2.2 Task', and not a table, and not a table of contents page"}]
